title: wedelia jacquinii subsp. cruciana
intro: Wedelia jacquinii subsp. cruciana, also known as the Wedelia or Mexican daisy, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the sunflower family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Wedelia jacquinii subsp. cruciana include its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate digestive issues, among other uses. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be used as a topical application to soothe skin irritations.
